channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze halted a crucial program fighting HIV in Africa. Here's what's at stake
item.description: The freeze on U.S. foreign aid ordered by President Donald Trump has effectively halted one of the world's most successful responses to a disease.
item.dc:creator: Farai Mutsaka And Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on remote Australian beach
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that became stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A worried NATO holds large-scale combat drills as the US stance on Europe shifts under Trump
item.description: NATO members are continuing their largest combat exercises of 2025 amid concern within the 32-nation alliance over its most powerful member, the United States.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Mcgrath And Nicolae Dumitrache, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump 'lives' in Russian 'disinformation space': Zelensky
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky responded to President Trump's latest rebuke, calling out Trump for living in a Russian "disinformation space.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: EU envoys approve more sanctions against Russia to mark the third anniversary of its war on Ukraine
item.description: European Union envoys have approved new sanctions against Russia, with the measures set to enter into force next week on the third anniversary of its full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Police in disputed Kashmir raid bookstores, seizing books related to Islamic group
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Police in Indian-controlled Kashmir raided bookstores and seized hundreds of books linked to a major Islamic organization in the disputed region, where strict controls on the press have escalated in recent years. The raids began on Friday in Srinagar, the region’s main city.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pakistan steps up arrests of Afghans without papers. Kabul says it's a move to expel all refugees
item.description: Pakistani authorities have stepped up arrests of Afghan citizens in Islamabad and the nearby city of Rawalpindi and are forcing them to move elsewhere in Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: Munir Ahmed, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Philippine village battles dengue by offering bounties for mosquitos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A village in the densely populated Philippine capital region launched a battle against dengue Wednesday by offering a token bounty to residents for captured mosquitos — dead or alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nearly 300 deportees from US held in Panama hotel as officials try to return them to their countries
item.description: Panama is detaining in a hotel nearly 300 people from various countries deported by the United States, not allowing them to leave while waiting for international authorities to organize a return to their countries.
item.dc:creator: Juan Zamorano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas says it will free 6 living hostages and hand over 4 bodies, accelerating Gaza releases
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A top Hamas official says the militant group will free six living Israeli hostages on Saturday and return the bodies of four others on Thursday, a surprise acceleration in releases apparently in trade for Israel’s allowing mobile homes and construction equipment into the devastated Gaza
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany's economic minister pushes for European X, end of Silicon Valley dominance
item.description: Robert Habeck, Germany’s economic minister and vice chancellor, pushed for Europe to create its own version of the social media platform X and urged the continent to break away from Silicon Valley’s dominance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 5 things to know about US-Russia talks on Ukraine
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s meeting with his Russian counterpart in Riyadh on Tuesday marked a major shift in U.S. relations with the country, ending a policy of isolation imposed after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: French bill would ban headscarves in sport; Amnesty International says it's discriminatory
item.description: Amnesty International is urging French lawmakers to reject a bill to ban religious clothing and symbols, including headscarves, during competitions, saying it is discriminatory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez says Vance 'lied to the world' about free speech
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) pressed Vice President Vance over the precepts of free speech, citing threats from the Trump administration as an example of its impediment. “@JDVance, you lied to the world in Munich.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: UN human rights chief accuses Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo of killing, recruiting children
item.description: The U.N. human rights chief accused Rwanda-backed rebels in Congo of killing children and attacking hospitals and warehouses storing humanitarian aid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border town's residents rebuild in south Lebanon as Hezbollah leader calls for Israeli withdrawal
item.description: KHIAM, Lebanon (AP) — Sabah Abdullah comes to her hometown in Lebanon every morning and sits next to her destroyed home. She is waiting for experts from Hezbollah's construction arm to compensate her for the damage caused by the Israel-Hezbollah war that has left her homeless.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea slams US and Asian rivals for pursuing 'absurd' plans to denuclearize North
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Tuesday criticized the United States and Asian neighbors for pursuing the “absurd” goal of denuclearizing the North and said it will push to expand its nuclear forces under the direction of its authoritarian leader Kim Jong Un.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Delta jet crashes at Canada's Toronto Pearson Airport; crews responding to plane flipped upside down
item.description: Officials confirmed to Fox News Digital that Delta plane crashed at Toronto Pearson Airport on Monday afternoon. The CRJ-900 jet, operating as Delta 4819, departed from Minneapolis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy not yet signing US economic agreement 'short-sighted,' White House official says
item.description: A White House national security official said Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "is being short-sighted" in not signing a rare earth minerals deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: World's first openly gay imam shot dead in South Africa: authorities
item.description: Muhsin Hendricks, a man known as the world's first openly gay imam, was killed in South Africa over the weekend after being ambushed near the city of Gqeberha.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels breach second major city in Congo's mineral-rich east
item.description: M23 rebels have reportedly "occupied" Bukavu, Democratic Republic of the Congo, positioning themselves at the governor's office and vowing to deviate from the "old regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gold mine collapse kills 42 in Mali
item.description: Late on Saturday, Malian television announced the collapse of the site at Bilali Koto in the commune of Dabia, with a provisional death toll of 42 and many injured.
item.dc:creator: BABA AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants name 3 Israeli men to be freed in next hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group on Friday welcomed the “joyous news” that three men, all taken from the same kibbutz on Oct. 7, 2023, are expected to be freed on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners held in Israeli jails.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Modi meet at Oval Office as US weighs tariffs
item.description: Before speaking with Trump, Modi met separately with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, a Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9
